text:
CT CHEST / ABDOMEN / PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
Restaging Examination

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma. Status post prior pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure) with adjuvant gemcitabine/capecitabine, now with biopsy-confirmed hepatic and suspected pulmonary metastatic recurrence. Currently on palliative systemic chemotherapy with gemcitabine plus nab-paclitaxel (Abraxane), completing 3 cycles. Restaging to assess treatment response.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following intravenous administration of 100 mL of iodinated contrast (Omnipaque 350) with acquisition in the portal venous phase. Axial images were obtained at 2.5 mm slice thickness through the chest and 3 mm through the abdomen and pelvis, with coronal and sagittal multiplanar reconstructions. Comparison is made to the prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis obtained approximately 3 months ago (the study demonstrating new hepatic and pulmonary lesions) and to the post-operative baseline CT obtained prior to adjuvant chemotherapy initiation.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: Lung volumes are preserved. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. There is no endobronchial lesion.

Pulmonary Nodules: A right lower lobe pulmonary nodule in the posterior basal segment measures approximately 7 mm in greatest dimension, unchanged compared to the prior examination. A left lower lobe pulmonary nodule in the posteromedial basal segment measures approximately 5 mm in greatest dimension, also unchanged. Neither nodule demonstrates internal cavitation, calcification, or spiculation. No new pulmonary nodules are identified. No consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or pleural-based masses.

Pleurae: No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pneumothorax.

Pericardium and Heart: The cardiac silhouette is within normal limits for size. No pericardial effusion.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinum is midline. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. No anterior mediastinal mass. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber. No thyroid lesion is visible on this limited assessment.

Chest Wall and Soft Tissues: No axillary lymphadenopathy. No chest wall masses. No osseous lesion suspicious for metastasis in the visualized thoracic spine or ribs.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and contour. Post-surgical changes from the prior hepaticojejunostomy are again noted at the hepatic hilum. A hypodense lesion is present in hepatic segment VI measuring approximately 1.2 cm in greatest dimension, unchanged compared to the prior study at which it measured 1.2 cm. This lesion demonstrates peripheral heterogeneous enhancement on the portal venous phase image, similar to prior. No new focal hepatic lesions are identified. There is no biliary ductal dilatation. The hepatic vasculature is patent.

Gallbladder: The gallbladder is surgically absent (cholecystectomy performed as part of the Whipple procedure), consistent with operative history.

Spleen: The spleen is normal in size, measuring approximately 11 cm in long axis. No splenic lesions.

Pancreas: The pancreatic body and tail remnant are visible. Post-pancreaticoduodenectomy anatomy is evident with absence of the pancreatic head. A small amount of linear peripancreatic fibrotic stranding persists at the pancreaticojejunostomy anastomotic site, stable and consistent with expected post-surgical scarring. No new peripancreatic fluid collection. No new soft tissue mass or recurrent tumor in the surgical bed. No pancreatic ductal dilatation in the remnant tail.

Adrenal Glands: Both adrenal glands appear normal without nodular enlargement or suspicious lesion.

Kidneys: Both kidneys are normal in size and enhancement pattern. No hydronephrosis. No renal mass. A small cortical cyst is present in the right kidney, too small to characterize but stable.

Stomach and Gastrointestinal Tract: Post-surgical anatomy is consistent with the pylorus-preserving pancreaticoduodenectomy with gastrojejunostomy reconstruction. The gastric remnant and jejunal loop are unremarkable. No small bowel obstruction or wall thickening. The visualized colon is without significant abnormality.

Retroperitoneum and Vascular Structures: The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber without aneurysmal dilatation. The celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and portal vein are patent without thrombosis. The superior mesenteric vein is patent, and there is no recurrent tumor encasement or extrinsic compression identified in the surgical bed. No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. The para-aortic and aortocaval lymph node stations are clear.

Peritoneal Cavity: No ascites. No peritoneal nodularity or thickening to suggest implants. The omentum appears unremarkable.

---

PELVIS:

Pelvic Organs: Uterus and ovaries are atrophic, as expected for a patient of this age. No adnexal mass. No free pelvic fluid.

Lymph Nodes: No pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

Bladder: The bladder is partially filled and unremarkable in appearance.

Osseous Structures: No lytic or sclerotic bony lesions suspicious for osseous metastasis in the visualized lumbar spine, sacrum, or iliac bones. Degenerative changes are present in the lower lumbar spine, including mild facet arthropathy at L4-L5 and L5-S1.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Hepatic segment VI lesion: Unchanged at approximately 1.2 cm, consistent with known biopsy-confirmed metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma. No interval growth or development of additional hepatic deposits.

2. Pulmonary nodules: Both the right lower lobe nodule (7 mm) and left lower lobe nodule (5 mm) are stable in size without interval progression. These are presumed to represent metastatic foci given the oncologic context, though no new nodules have appeared.

3. No new sites of metastatic disease identified. No peritoneal implants. No lymphadenopathy. No new osseous lesions.

4. Post-surgical changes from pancreaticoduodenectomy are stable and unremarkable without evidence of locoregional recurrence or anastomotic complication.

Overall, the constellation of findings is consistent with disease stability across all known sites of metastatic involvement following 3 cycles of gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel therapy. No disease progression is identified on this examination.

Serum tumor markers at time of this study per clinical team: CA 19-9 45 U/mL (previously 67 U/mL); CEA 3.9 ng/mL (previously 4.8 ng/mL).
